system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet duct (x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25)
    (-50,25,25)// 0
    (-50,25,-25)// 1
    (-50,-25,-25)// 2
    (-50,-25,25)// 3

    (0,25,25)// 4
    (0,25,-25)// 5
    (0,-25,-25)// 6
    (0,-25,25)// 7

    // Block 1 - floor bend region
    (25,-25,25)// 8
    (25,-25,-25)// 9
    (25,0,25)// 10
    (25,0,-25)// 11

    // Block 2 - ceiling bend region
    (25,25,25)// 12
    (25,25,-25)// 13

    // Block 3 - outlet upper duct (x=25..-500, y=-75..-25, z=-25..25)
    (25,-75,25)// 14
    (25,-75,-25)// 15

    // Block 4 - outlet lower duct (x=25..-500, y=-25..-75, z=-25..25)
    (-500,-75,25)// 16
    (-500,-75,-25)// 17
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // Block 0
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)      (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // Block 1
    hex (4 7 11 5 12 15 10 8)    (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // Block 2
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)  (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) // Block 3
    hex (12 15 16 10 14 17 18 13)(100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) // Block 4
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)      // Block 0 top
            (4 12 13 5)    // Block 2 top
            (12 14 15 13)  // Block 3 top
            (14 16 17 15)  // Block 4 top
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)      // Block 0 bottom
            (11 10 8 7)    // Block 1 bottom
            (10 14 15 11)  // Block 4 bottom
            (15 18 19 10)  // Block 4 bottom (second face)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2)      // Block 0 left
            (9 8 10 11)    // Block 1 left
            (13 12 8 10)   // Block 2 left
            (15 12 16 13)  // Block 4 left
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)      // Block 0 right
            (11 4 8 10)    // Block 2 right
            (10 15 14 11)  // Block 3 right
            (10 18 19 13)  // Block 4 right
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

    in file lnInclude/Scalar.C at line 101.

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Token parsing error

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 15 the punctuation token ','
```

**Diagnosis:**

Despite applying fixes in Attempt 6, the same parsing error persists at line 15. The issue is that the vertex numbering in the `vertices` list creates a structural problem. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict` carefully, the problem is specifically with vertex indexing and how the blocks reference them.

The fundamental issue is that the block topology references vertex indices that don't form valid planar faces. The error at line 15 suggests the parser is choking on the vertex definition itself, likely due to having too many vertices defined with comments that include parentheses or formatting that triggers the CSV parser incorrectly.

**Resolution:**

Adopt a minimal, clean vertex definition format without any comments mixed into the vertex data. Rewrite the entire `vertices` section as:

```
vertices
(
    (-50, 25, 25)
    (-50, 25, -25)
    (-50, -25, -25)
    (-50, -25, 25)

    (0, 25, 25)
    (0, 25, -25)
    (0, -25, -25)
    (0, -25, 25)

    (25, -25, 25)
    (25, -25, -25)
    (25, 0, 25)
    (25, 0, -25)

    (25, 25, 25)
    (25, 25, -25)

    (25, -75, 25)
    (25, -75, -25)

    (-500, -75, 25)
    (-500, -75, -25)
);
```

No comments anywhere in the vertex list. Then update the block definitions to use the correct vertex indices based on this new ordering (0-19):

- **Block 0** (inlet duct): `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)`
- **Block 1** (floor bend): `hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)`
- **Block 2** (ceiling bend): `hex (4 7 11 5 12 15 10 8)`
- **Block 3** (outlet upper): `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)`
- **Block 4** (outlet lower): `hex (12 15 14 10 16 19 18 13)`

Update all boundary face definitions to match the new vertex ordering. The boundary patches (`inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`) must reference the correct vertex indices from the newly defined blocks.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

**Diagnosis:**

This is entirely dependent on `blockMesh` succeeding. Once the mesh is successfully generated, this error disappears.

## Summary

The persistent parsing error stems from how OpenFOAM reads the `vertices` section. Even with seemingly correct formatting, the presence of comments or specific spacing triggers the CSV-like tokenizer to fail. The fix is to provide a completely barebones `vertices` list with **zero comments** and **minimal whitespace**, then rebuild the entire block topology and boundary definitions to match the resulting vertex indices. All other files (controlDict, BCs, schemes, etc.) remain correct and unchanged.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire vertices list with clean format without any comments; Redefine blocks with correct vertex indices matching new order: Block 0 hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5), Block 1 hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11), Block 2 hex (4 7 11 5 12 15 10 8), Block 3 hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15), Block 4 hex (12 15 14 10 16 19 18 13); Update all boundary face definitions to match new vertex ordering: inlet face (0 1 2 3), outlet face (16 17 18 19), upperWall faces (0 4 5 1) (4 12 13 5) (12 14 15 13) (14 16 17 15), lowerWall faces (3 7 6 2) (11 10 8 7) (10 14 15 11) (15 18 19 10), leftWall faces (1 5 6 2) (9 8 10 11) (13 12 8 10) (15 12 16 13), rightWall faces (0 3 7 4) (11 4 8 10) (10 15 14 11) (10 18 19 13)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
